text.title: stakeholder-engagement Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for stakeholder-engagement as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Auto-sending communications to executives, regulators, or board members without PM approval.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: These stakeholders are high-stakes; an error in an automated message to an executive or regulator has disproportionate reputation and legal consequences. Agent drafts; PM or sponsor approves.

failure-mode.description: Conflating stakeholder (real named person with decision power) with persona (synthesised archetype).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Stakeholders require personalised engagement plans; personas are for UX design. Mixing them produces engagement plans that address a fictional composite rather than real decision-makers.

failure-mode.description: Applying Western-norm engagement defaults to high-context cultures (JP, KR, parts of EU/MEA).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Influence routes through indirect networks in high-context cultures; a direct weekly 1:1 invitation to a Japanese executive may be perceived as disrespectful. Add cultural_context field and adjust approach.
